question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
## `charmcraft.yaml` Structure for a Kubernetes Charm

Here's a complete, annotated example of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes (sidecar) charm:

```yaml
# charmcraft.yaml

# ── Identity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
name: my-app                        # charm name (must match metadata.yaml if split)
type: charm                         # always "charm" for operator charms

# ── Runtime / bases ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
bases:
  - build-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"
    run-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"

# ── Charm metadata (can live here OR in a separate metadata.yaml) ─────────────
title: My Application
summary: A one-line description of the charm.
description: |
  A longer description of what this charm deploys and manages.
  Supports **Markdown**.

# Kubernetes workload containers (required for K8s/sidecar charms)
containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image          # must reference an OCI resource below
    mounts:
      - storage: data               # must reference a storage entry below
        location: /var/lib/my-app

# OCI image resources
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the my-app workload container
    upstream-source: docker.io/myorg/my-app:latest   # default / upstream ref

# Storage
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent data directory
    minimum-size: 1G

# Relations
requires:
  db:
    interface: pgsql
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1

provides:
  metrics-endpoint:
    interface: prometheus_scrape

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-peers

# Config options exposed via `juju config`
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: INFO
      description: Python log level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR)
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on

# Extra bindings (optional)
extra-bindings:
  public:

# ── Build config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
parts:
  my-app:
    plugin: charm                   # uses the built-in charm plugin
    build-snaps:
      - rustup                      # optional: extra snaps needed at build time
    charm-binary-python-packages:
      - cryptography                # packages built from source (C extensions etc.)
    charm-python-packages:          # pure-Python packages added to the venv
      - boto3
    charm-requirements:             # pip requirements files
      - requirements.txt

# ── Actions (optional) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  rotate-credentials:
    description: Rotate application credentials without downtime.
    params:
      force:
        type: boolean
        default: false
        description: Skip safety checks.

# ── Assumes (optional, guards minimum Juju version / features) ────────────────
assumes:
  - juju >= 3.3
  - k8s-api                        # declares this charm needs the K8s API
```

---

## Key Sections Explained

| Section | Required? | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | ✅ | Unique charm name on Charmhub |
| `type: charm` | ✅ | Identifies this as an operator charm |
| `bases` | ✅ | Ubuntu channels to build/run on |
| `containers` | ✅ K8s | Declares sidecar workload containers |
| `resources` | ✅ K8s | OCI image(s) referenced by `containers` |
| `parts` | ✅ | Charmcraft build instructions (plugin, deps) |
| `summary` / `description` | ✅ | Human-readable charm metadata |
| `config` | Optional | User-configurable charm options |
| `relations` | Optional | Integration endpoints |
| `storage` | Optional | Persistent volumes |
| `actions` | Optional | Operator runbook actions |
| `assumes` | Optional | Minimum Juju version / feature guards |

---

## Important K8s-Specific Rules

1. **Every `containers` entry** must reference an `oci-image` resource via `resource:`.
2. **Mounts** in `containers` must reference a named `storage` block.
3. Use `assumes: [k8s-api]` if your charm calls the Kubernetes API directly (e.g., via `lightkube`).
4. The charm's `dispatch` script (generated by the `charm` plugin) will be called by the Juju K8s agent inside the **charm container**, not the workload container.
